---
title: NCBI
alias: &ALIAS ncbi
href: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI) is a U.S. National Library of Medicine resource providing access to biomedical and genomic information, including databases like PubMed, GenBank, and Entrez.

## Key Features

- Hosts PubMed, GenBank, RefSeq, dbSNP, and dozens of other interconnected biomedical databases
- Provides BLAST sequence alignment tools and Entrez cross-database search infrastructure
- Offers programmatic API access via the Entrez Programming Utilities (E-utilities) and NCBI Datasets
- Supports sequence submission, annotation pipelines, and INSDC data sharing standards
- Regularly updated with curated reference genomes, protein records, and clinical variant data

## Use Cases in Research

- Retrieving reference genome assemblies and annotated gene sequences for pathogen analysis
- Mining PubMed literature for AMR, microbiome, or pharmacological evidence synthesis
- Downloading SRA/GEO datasets for transcriptomic and metagenomic re-analysis pipelines
- Identifying SNPs and structural variants associated with antimicrobial resistance phenotypes
- Building training datasets for machine learning models using curated NCBI records

## Getting Started

Visit [NCBI](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
